CropNutrientMetabolismGeneExtraction.title: CropNutrientMetabolismGeneExtraction
CropNutrientMetabolismGeneExtraction.description:
Template: Crop nutrient metabolism gene extraction (v1.0)

Goal: extract key genes (enzymes + regulators) that control the presence/yield/content of final nutritional products in crops.
Architecture: Study -> Multiple Genes (each gene contains species info).
Constraint: Within each gene dictionary, each key's value may be empty, a string, a number, or a list. Do NOT nest lists/dictionaries inside each other.
Field names preserve original CSV capitalization.
All optional string fields default to "NA".

NutrientMetabolismGene.title: NutrientMetabolismGene
NutrientMetabolismGene.description:
Individual gene object for crop nutrient metabolism studies.

Covers enzyme-coding genes (rate-limiting/branch/transport/storage) and regulatory genes (TFs/miRNAs/signaling) that shift flux to final nutrient products.

Gene_Name.description: Gene symbol/name used in the paper (e.g., OsNAS2, ZmPSY1, SlGGP1, AtVTE2).

Species.description: Crop species common name (e.g., Rice, Maize, Wheat, Tomato).

Species_Latin_Name.description: Latin name (e.g., Oryza sativa, Zea mays, Triticum aestivum).

Reference_Genome_Version.description: Reference genome assembly/version used for coordinates if reported.

Chromosome_Position.description: Gene genomic interval or coordinate (e.g., Chr1:12345–13579). Exclude variant positions.

Gene_Role_Class.description: Controlled vocabulary recommended: Enzyme; Transporter; Storage/Binding; Regulatory_TF; Regulatory_RNA; Signaling; Other.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.description: Protein family/domain (e.g., bHLH TF, SDR, cytochrome P450, MATE transporter).

Enzyme_Name_or_Function.description: If enzyme gene: enzyme name or biochemical function (e.g., phytoene synthase; GDP-L-galactose phosphorylase). If regulator: brief function (e.g., activates carotenoid pathway genes).

Final_Nutrient_Product.description: The final nutritional product whose presence/yield/content is the terminal trait (e.g., β-carotene, folate, vitamin E tocopherols, vitamin C ascorbate, lysine, anthocyanins, iron in grain, zinc in endosperm).

Final_Nutrient_Product_Class.description: Broad nutrient class: Vitamin; Mineral; Amino_Acid; Fatty_Acid; Carotenoid; Polyphenol/Flavonoid; Alkaloid; Fiber/Carbohydrate; Other.

Nutrient_Trait_Metric.description: What was measured for the final product: content; concentration; yield per plant/area; bioavailability proxy; speciation; stability; retention after processing.

Quantitative_Nutrient_Change.description: Numeric effect size for final nutrient product (e.g., +30%, 2.1-fold, +5.2 µg/g DW).

Units_and_Basis.description: Units and basis used (e.g., µg/g DW, mg/100g FW, nmol/g, ppm; seed/grain/endosperm).

Pathway_Name.description: Metabolic pathway(s) involved (e.g., carotenoid biosynthesis, folate biosynthesis, Fe homeostasis, ascorbate pathway, amino acid biosynthesis).

Metabolic_Step_Type.description: Recommended vocabulary: Committed_Step; Rate_Limiting; Branch_Point; Terminal_Step; Recycling/Salvage; Transport; Storage/Sequestration; Regulation.

Reaction_Substrate.description: Primary substrate(s) if reported (free text, comma-separated).

Reaction_Product_or_Immediate_Output.description: Immediate reaction product(s) or transported species (free text, comma-separated).

Key_Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected.description: Intermediate metabolites whose levels change (e.g., GGPP; phytoene; γ-tocopherol; nicotianamine). Use a list of strings if multiple.

Flux_or_Direction_Change.description: How metabolism is redirected (e.g., increases flux toward β-carotene branch; reduces competing lignin branch; enhances Fe translocation to grain).

Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient.description: Most important conclusion linking gene perturbation/allele to final nutrient product outcome.

Regulatory_Mechanism.description: Mechanism: transcriptional activation/repression, phosphorylation, feedback inhibition, protein stability, complex formation, etc.

Regulatory_Relationships.description: Gene network edges in a flat string format: Upstream->Regulation_Type->Downstream, using '->' for activation/promotion, '--|' for inhibition. Separate multiple by ';'. Example: TF1->activates->PSY1; HY5--|represses--|X.

Interacting_Proteins.description: Experimentally validated interactors (protein/gene IDs). Use list of strings or ';' delimited string.

Subcellular_Localization.description: Localization relevant to metabolism (chloroplast/plastid, ER, cytosol, vacuole, mitochondria, apoplast, nucleus). Include method if noted (e.g., GFP).

Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation.description: Where the final nutrient product is quantified/accumulates: grain endosperm, embryo, leaf, tuber, fruit pericarp, etc.

Developmental_Stage.description: Stage for sampling/phenotype (e.g., milk stage grain, mature seed, breaker fruit).

Expression_Pattern.description: Tissues/conditions with high expression; include inducible cues (e.g., Fe deficiency, nitrogen supply, light).

Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.description: Environmental/treatment factors influencing nutrient metabolism (e.g., Fe deficiency, sulfate limitation, light intensity, post-harvest storage, cooking/processing).

Experimental_Materials.description: Mutant/line type and background (e.g., CRISPR knockout, OE line, NIL, RNAi).

Core_Validation_Method.description: Most critical functional validation experiments (e.g., knockout/knockdown/OE; complementation; enzyme assay; feeding labeling; transient expression).

Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method.description: How nutrient/metabolites were measured: HPLC, LC-MS/MS, GC-MS, ICP-MS/ICP-OES (minerals), colorimetric assay, targeted metabolomics, untargeted metabolomics.

Omics_Data.description: Omics types and accessions if reported (RNA-seq, metabolomics, proteomics, GWAS, resequencing).

Key_Variant_Type.description: Variant type if causal allele reported: SNP; Indel; CNV; PAV; Structural_Variant.

Key_Variant_Site.description: Causal variant/haplotype marker as described (e.g., g.123A>T; promoter InDel).

Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype.description: Allele/haplotype associated with higher nutrient product content or desired nutrient profile.

Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method.description: How the gene was identified: map-based cloning; GWAS; QTL; candidate; comparative genomics; omics-driven screening; CRISPR screen.

Genetic_Population.description: Population used for association/QTL if any (e.g., RILs, F2, diversity panel).

Field_Trials_or_GxE.description: Field trial and/or GxE notes relevant to nutrient outcome (location/season; soil nutrient status).

Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value.description: Applied value: marker-assisted selection; biofortification; processing stability; stacking suggestions; trade-offs.

Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.description: Main findings in numbered format: '1. ... 2. ... 3. ...'. Focus on nutrient metabolism causality.

Notes_or_Other_Important_Info.description: Anything crucial not captured elsewhere (e.g., epistasis, pathway redundancy, processing losses, cultivar specificity).

DOI.description: Pure DOI string, no URL prefix.

Genes.title: Genes
Genes.description: List of key gene objects (enzymes/regulators) impacting final nutrient products.

example.Title: Engineering carotenoid flux to increase provitamin A content in maize endosperm

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux into carotenoid backbone synthesis, raising downstream β-carotene.
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Overexpression increases β-carotene accumulation in endosperm.
Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Candidate for biofortification; potential for stacking with downstream cyclase/hydroxylase edits.
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPSY1 enhances committed entry into carotenoid biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression increases provitamin A (β-carotene) in endosperm. 3. Intermediate carotenoids also increase, consistent with higher pathway flux.
